hypothesis: If adaptive diversity constraint algorithms are applied during reinforcement-learning generation of organic electronic molecules, then the resulting library will show higher diversity across representation-based, fragment-based, scaffold-based, and SMILES-pattern metrics while maintaining or improving the rate at which generated molecules meet the singlet fission energy criterion ΔE_SF = E(S1) − 2·E(T1) below the target threshold, compared with baseline unconstrained generation.
